summary: There is insufficient high-quality longitudinal evidence comparing long-term outcomes including continence, recurrence, and quality of life between transanal and transabdominal pull-through procedures for Hirschsprung disease. As a result, it cannot be concluded that they produce equally successful long-term results due to significant data gaps in durable performance assessment.
